# Transcrição: 03-How to Check if the Pixel is Installed Correctly 

- **Fonte:** 03-How to Check if the Pixel is Installed Correctly 
- **Duração:** 03:43
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:04]

Hey guys, this video is going to be about how to make sure that your Facebook pixel is tracking and firing correctly. So this is going to be using an extension on the Google Chrome browser called Facebook Pixel Helper. So this is an image of what it looks like, what the app is called, and then how you can find it. You just need to go to your extensions tab on your Google Chrome browser and just search for Facebook Pixel Helper. You'll be able to find that and then install it onto your Google Chrome browser. Beyond that, this is how you use it. So you can see here it pops up and on certain sites it'll pop up and tell you exactly what's firing, what events are firing, and if it's firing properly. So you can see here, if we go to this as an example, this is movement watches. So we're on a product page. So this product page, it should be firing for page view as well as view content, right? Because view content is firing for a specific product. And this is a specific product, so they're on that product page. And you can see if you go up here, you can see the drop down, it's firing for page view and it's firing for view content. And it's got a little green check mark there as well. So that's an indication that it's installed properly and these are the events that it's installed for. So here it's looking like it's, there's two pixels that are firing. Some sites do have multiple pixels. Some sites have multiple pixels because of some retargeting software they're using. So don't be super alarmed by that. If one of your clients or prospects has that, just check in with them and see what the situation is. Is it because they have multiple pixels? Is it because they're using a retargeting software? Or is it just because they're unaware of it and they might have another agency who has installed a Facebook pixel previously? So it's really important there to check and see which one of those options is the reason why they have two or three or four or multiple pixels on their site. Now beyond that, if it's not firing properly, you're going to see here for this as an example here. So this is Sage, not to pick apart these guys at all, but they don't have their Facebook pixel installed properly here. So you can see here they have three different pixels. Again, it could be for a number of reasons, but here none of the pixels are firing for view content. So this one is just firing for page view. This one is not firing for view content. And then this one is not firing for view content either. Or it's not linked up properly. So it's not linked up to a product catalog or something along those lines. So that's when if it's not linked up to a product catalog, it's going to give you this little yellow kind of warning sign. So if you click here, you can actually see what the warning is. So this one says the pixel is not paired with any product catalog. Okay, so that makes sense. That's typically what happens when it's firing, when it's giving you a little warning, this fire here, this one is just not paired to a product catalog, or it just won't show up

## [03:04 - 03:43]

at all. So if you can check here using the Facebook pixel helper, if there is a pixel on the site, just by going to the site and using this extension and just clicking on the drop down. So not only that, but you can also see when it's being fired as well. So here, if we click add to bag, it should add something to the cart and it should fire for add to cart. See here, it's firing for add to cart, but there's some sort of error. So this one, it says the recommended parameter value is missing. So it'll tell you exactly what's wrong with the pixel firing and you'll be able to change that accordingly. So that's how you can check and see if your Facebook pixel is installed correctly using the Google Chrome extension Facebook pixel helper.
